sections.heading: Surveillance
sections.content: { "clinical_follow_up_schedule": ["No explicit, standardized follow-up schedule is provided in the NCCN Guidelines for pleural mesothelioma. Follow-up frequency and intensity are determined by the treating multidisciplinary team based on the treatment received, disease status, and patient factors."], "imaging_strategy": ["Chest/abdomen CT with contrast is used for initial staging [MS-5].", "FDG-PET/CT may be used for initial staging and to assess metabolic activity, ideally before pleurodesis [MS-5].", "Response assessment and surveillance typically involve periodic CT scans, though the exact interval is not mandated."], "laboratory_monitoring": ["Soluble mesothelin-related peptide (SMRP) is an optional biomarker that may correlate with disease status and be used for monitoring [PM-1, MS-3].", "Complete blood counts and metabolic panels are monitored during systemic therapy to assess toxicity."], "supportive_follow_up": ["Ongoing assessment and management of symptoms (dyspnea, pain).", "Reassessment of nutritional status and psychosocial distress.", "Monitoring for and managing long-term sequelae of treatment (e.g., pulmonary fibrosis, cardiac issues)."] }

timeline.title: Initial Staging
timeline.body: Chest/abdomen CT with contrast is used for initial staging [MS-5]. FDG-PET/CT may be used for initial staging and to assess metabolic activity, ideally before pleurodesis [MS-5].

timeline.title: During Systemic Therapy
timeline.body: Complete blood counts and metabolic panels are monitored during systemic therapy to assess toxicity. Soluble mesothelin-related peptide (SMRP) is an optional biomarker that may correlate with disease status and be used for monitoring [PM-1, MS-3].

timeline.title: Ongoing Surveillance
timeline.body: Response assessment and surveillance typically involve periodic CT scans, though the exact interval is not mandated. Ongoing assessment and management of symptoms (dyspnea, pain). Reassessment of nutritional status and psychosocial distress. Monitoring for and managing long-term sequelae of treatment (e.g., pulmonary fibrosis, cardiac issues).

timeline.title: Follow-up Schedule
timeline.body: No explicit, standardized follow-up schedule is provided in the NCCN Guidelines for pleural mesothelioma. Follow-up frequency and intensity are determined by the treating multidisciplinary team based on the treatment received, disease status, and patient factors.
